channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump reaches deals with 5 law firms, allowing them to avoid prospect of punishing executive orders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced deals Friday with five law firms that will allow them to avoid the prospect of punishing executive orders and require them to together provide hundreds of millions of dollars' worth of free legal services for causes his administration says it suppor
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dem states to blame for most of the nearly $400 million in unemployment fraud over last 4 years, DOGE says
item.description: DOGE found that three states, all dominated by Democratic Party control, accounted for most of the hundreds of millions of dollars in unemployment fraud seen over the last four years under former President Biden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Dems spar with whistleblower who exposed children's hospital for performing trans surgeries on minors
item.description: Whistleblower Dr. Eithan Haim faced tough questions from House Democrats after he leaked medical documents exposing transgender medical procedures at a Texas children's hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump opens window for a deal with Iran but issues warning if things don't work out
item.description: President Donald Trump is betting that a beleaguered Iran is so vulnerable following a tumultuous 18 months in the Middle East that it might finally be ready to abandon its nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Picture of Trump after the assassination attempt displaces Obama portrait at the White House
item.description: The White House has hung a painting of President Donald Trump depicting a moment after an assassination attempt against him last summer — bumping the official portrait of one of his predecessors, Barack Obama.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville And Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: What to know about Dan Caine, the nation's newly-confirmed top military adviser
item.description: The Senate voted around 2 a.m. Friday to confirm retired Air Force Lt. Gen. Dan Caine as chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff. Trump plucked him from retirement to be his top military adviser.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, Juliana Kim, and Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Trump announces $600 million in new deals with five law firms
item.description: The deals bring to nine the number of firms that have struck agreements with Trump in the wake of numerous actions targeting major law offices.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Iowa Republican Gov. Kim Reynolds announces she won't seek reelection in 2026
item.description: In a surprise announcement Friday, Iowa Gov. Kim Reynolds said she will not seek a third term in office in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge scolds DOJ for demanding delay in mistakenly deported man case
item.description: The federal judge who ordered the Trump administration to try to return Kilmar Abrego Garcia, the man mistakenly deported to El Salvador, scolded the Justice Department for demanding more time to update the court on next steps.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens to put higher tariff rate back if countries don’t strike deals
item.description: President Trump on Thursday threatened to reverse course on a 90-day pause on country-specific tariffs if countries don’t strike a deal with his administration before then.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: NRCC launches ad campaign targeting dozens of vulnerable Dems who voted against key Trump proposal
item.description: The NRCC has launched an ad campaign targeting over 20 vulnerable Democrats who voted against a House resolution in support of President Donald Trump's budget agenda.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tariff reversal may have enriched cronies: Schiff
item.description: One of President Trump’s top political rivals, Democratic Sen. Adam Schiff of California, is demanding an investigation.
item.dc:creator: Blake Burman, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court says Trump administration must work to bring back mistakenly deported Maryland man
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Thursday said the Trump administration must work to bring back a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported to prison in El Salvador, rejecting the administration’s emergency appeal.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Where things stand for Trump in global tariff battle
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the aftermath of this week's tariff whiplash, President Donald Trump is deciding exactly what he wants out of trade talks with as many as 75 nations in the coming weeks. Trump is also figuring out next steps with China.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge allows requirement that everyone in the US illegally must register to move forward
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday allowed the Trump administration to move forward with a requirement that everyone in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Las Vegas movie studio plan changing with new players, tech/education emphasis
item.description: A global player, MBS Group, is stepping into the plan for a $900 million movie studio at the UNLV Harry Reid Research and Technology Park, near the 215 Beltway and Durango Drive in the southwest Las Vegas valley. Birtcher Development LLC is the project's developer.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court rules Trump admin must bring Kilmar Abrego Garcia back to US
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court upheld on Thursday a judge's order requiring President Donald Trump's administration to facilitate the return to the United States of a Salvadoran man who the government has acknowledged was deported in error to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: By John Kruzel and Andrew Chung / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Stocks slide as Trump's trade war with China spooks Wall Street
item.description: Stocks slid Thursday as President Trump’s escalating trade war with China and concerns about its economic implications continued to rattle Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DOJ moves to drop charges against man it labeled MS-13 leader
item.description: The move to dismiss the charges prompted the unusual push from Villatoro Santos’s lawyer seeking to delay the dropping of any charges against his client.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans pass bill requiring proof of citizenship to vote
item.description: House Republicans for a second time passed a stand-alone bill that would require proof of citizenship to vote in federal elections and impose voter roll purge requirements on states. The legislation — formally titled the Safeguard American Voter Eligibility (SAVE) Act — passed in a 220-208 vote.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Boozy battle: Maryland and Delaware fight over 'Orange Crush' cocktail
item.description: Lawmakers in Maryland voted Monday to approve the orange crush as the Old Line state's official state cocktail.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: US trade representative testifies before House on Trump trade agenda
item.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer testified before the House Ways and Means Committee on Wednesday morning on President Trump's trade agenda, as the latest reciprocal tariffs took effect overnight.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries accuses Trump of tanking the economy to create opportunities for the rich
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Monday accused President Trump of intentionally crashing the economy to benefit the GOP’s wealthy donors and other well-heeled interests.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tariffs pose danger for vulnerable Republicans
item.description: Vulnerable House Republicans are being put on defense amid the fallout from President Donald Trump’s sweeping global tariffs, which have stoked economic uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, Fox 5
